prerequisites: A Google Cloud project with the BigQuery API enabled. Supply its ID in the required project_id field.
A BigQuery dataset to receive synced data, referenced by the required dataset_id field.
A dataset_location (region or multi-region) chosen at creation. The location cannot be changed afterward and all related datasets should share it.
A service account with the BigQuery User and BigQuery Data Editor IAM roles granted on the project or dataset.
For the GCS Staging loading method only: a Cloud Storage bucket plus the Storage Object Admin role on the service account, and an HMAC key (access ID and secret) for that bucket.

authMethods.name: Service account key (credentials_json)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate by pasting the full JSON contents of a Google Cloud service account key into the credentials_json field, including the surrounding braces. The service account must hold the BigQuery User and BigQuery Data Editor roles. Generate the key from the service account's Keys tab in the Google Cloud console (Add key > Create new key > JSON); the file downloads once and cannot be re-downloaded.

authMethods.name: GCS HMAC key (loading_method.credential)
authMethods.summary: Used only when loading_method is set to GCS Staging. Provide an HMAC key access ID (loading_method.credential.hmac_key_access_id) and secret (loading_method.credential.hmac_key_secret) created for the staging Cloud Storage bucket. The same service account also needs the Storage Object Admin role on the bucket. This is in addition to the credentials_json service account, not a replacement for it.

setupSteps.title: Prepare the BigQuery project and dataset
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud console, enable the BigQuery API on your project and note the project ID for project_id. Create a destination dataset and record its name for dataset_id and its region or multi-region for dataset_location. The dataset location cannot be changed later, so pick the region that matches your other datasets.

setupSteps.title: Create a service account and grant roles
setupSteps.body: Create a service account in the project and grant it the BigQuery User and BigQuery Data Editor IAM roles so it can run queries and write data. If you plan to use GCS Staging, also grant the Storage Object Admin role on the staging bucket.

setupSteps.title: Generate the service account key JSON
setupSteps.body: Open the service account's Keys tab in the Google Cloud console, click Add key > Create new key, choose JSON, and click Create to download the key file (it can only be downloaded once). Paste the entire JSON contents, including the braces, into the credentials_json field.

setupSteps.title: Choose a loading method
setupSteps.body: Leave loading_method at the default Batched Standard Inserts for the simplest setup, where BigQuery manages and auto-deletes upload files. For higher-volume loads, set loading_method to GCS Staging and supply the bucket along with the HMAC key access ID (loading_method.credential.hmac_key_access_id) and secret (loading_method.credential.hmac_key_secret).

setupSteps.title: Set optional behavior fields
setupSteps.body: Optionally set raw_data_dataset to override where raw tables are written, disable_type_dedupe to skip typing and deduplication, and cdc_deletion_mode to control how CDC deletions are handled. Leave these unset to use defaults.

sources.title: Airbyte BigQuery destination (setup, roles, loading methods)
